commitments.actor: Codex agent handling a Logic Writing request
commitments.expected_result: exactly one of investigation, academic-writing, fiction-writing, or travel-guide owns the requested terminal artifact
commitments.expected_terminal: one owner selected or a visible ambiguity/out-of-scope result
commitments.failure_boundary: never activate two final owners and never guess a materially ambiguous owner
commitments.label: select exactly one final owner
commitments.rationale: terminal deliverable ownership prevents duplicate orchestration
commitments.trigger: a non-trivial request enters Logic Writing
commitments.validation_boundary: route model, select-route contract, and routing tests cover the same decision

commitments.expected_result: typed native receipts are consumed without reimplementing specialist decisions
commitments.expected_terminal: current native receipt or visible provider/dependency failure
commitments.failure_boundary: missing specialists block substitution and degraded states remain visible
commitments.label: preserve specialist Guard authority
commitments.rationale: a shell may coordinate domain owners but may not become a shadow implementation

relations.rationale: the selected owner decides which specialist receipts are required

commitments.trigger: the final route needs source, logic, trace, document, PDF, or process evidence
commitments.validation_boundary: adapter contracts validate identity and status but do not rerun native checks

commitments.expected_result: a fingerprinted ResearchPacket preserves sources, support limits, alternatives, gaps, and native receipts, with current source-observation and argument-model evidence as its minimum content baseline
commitments.expected_terminal: current packet, bounded partial packet, or visible rejection
commitments.failure_boundary: candidate sources, plans, stale receipts, access gaps, and process-only green receipts cannot become passing content evidence
commitments.label: assemble a verifier-derived ResearchPacket
commitments.rationale: one typed handoff prevents research ledgers from leaking into prose or being silently strengthened

relations.rationale: packet status is derived from native receipt status

commitments.trigger: investigation has enough bounded evidence to hand off or report
commitments.validation_boundary: packet schema, member manifest, exact receipt identities, minimum content domains, and verifier-derived status

commitments.expected_result: a route-neutral ReaderBrief base plus the selected route extension carries audience, meaning, reader state, contribution sequence, evidence or canon boundaries, limitations, and wording constraints
commitments.failure_boundary: raw ledgers and internal Guard language never substitute for a reader brief
commitments.label: translate evidence into a ReaderBrief
commitments.rationale: the two-room boundary separates diagnostic machinery from reader-facing explanation

relations.rationale: the selected owner determines the route extension and admissible content packet

commitments.trigger: the final route is ready to plan reader-facing prose
commitments.validation_boundary: brief sanitizer retains evidence boundaries while excluding internal instructions

commitments.expected_result: deterministic and separately judged evidence inspect the actual current artifact for contribution, reader-state handoff, explanation pressure, register ownership, variation, model-artifact binding, and route-native semantics
commitments.expected_terminal: current pass, repair request, or visible not-run/degraded state
commitments.failure_boundary: metadata, outlines, or audits for an older fingerprint cannot prove prose quality
commitments.label: audit the actual reader artifact
commitments.rationale: quality claims must be bound to what the user receives

relations.rationale: artifact quality is checked against its reader contract

commitments.trigger: reader-facing text or document is materially complete
commitments.validation_boundary: actual text fingerprint, deterministic diagnostics, and independent judgment receipt

commitments.expected_result: only the selected final owner derives closure from the shared minimum baseline, current route-native content, reader evidence, child Guard receipts, and exact artifact identities
commitments.expected_terminal: passed, partial, blocked, or bounded no-progress termination with residual risk
commitments.failure_boundary: children, adapters, plans, self-reported status, and process-only green evidence cannot issue final closure; academic closure additionally requires current source-unit-to-target-unit revision provenance
commitments.label: derive final closure with one owner
commitments.rationale: verifier-derived monotonic closure prevents fake green completion
commitments.trigger: the final route attempts to finish the user artifact
commitments.validation_boundary: minimum route content, ReaderBrief, deterministic audit, independent reader judgment, academic revision provenance when applicable, and exact artifact identities

commitments.expected_result: one frozen validation owner materializes every governed source, every live consumer binds the archive-stable release contract, and the post-archive snapshot aggregates exact current child receipts
commitments.expected_terminal: immutable terminal success receipt or visible blocker
commitments.failure_boundary: progress, duplicate owners, mutable inputs, generated-output basename collisions, change-lifecycle contract paths, missing post-archive replay, and interrupted descendants never count as final evidence
commitments.label: freeze the final validation owner
commitments.rationale: one execution owner prevents duplicate or stale release evidence

relations.rationale: release validation covers the implementation that derives operation closure, not a user artifact instance

commitments.trigger: the release candidate source and toolchain identities are frozen
commitments.validation_boundary: one stable current verification contract, byte-identical frozen source materialization, finite generated-output and archive-lifecycle bad-case families, post-archive full replay, and immutable terminal receipts

commitments.expected_result: the exact validated projection is staged, checked, and activated with rollback retained
commitments.expected_terminal: active current installation or rollback to prior active state
commitments.failure_boundary: never mutate the active installation before stage verification
commitments.label: activate a recoverable Logic Writing installation
commitments.rationale: staging and rollback prevent replacement from destroying the current working route

relations.rationale: installation consumes the frozen installation projection

commitments.trigger: a validated release candidate is ready to install
commitments.validation_boundary: stage identity, activation identity, installed smoke, and rollback evidence

commitments.expected_result: exactly one current logic-writing route resolves globally
commitments.expected_terminal: one current route or visible routing block
commitments.failure_boundary: old route IDs and duplicate authorities cannot remain active
commitments.label: project one global Logic Writing route
commitments.rationale: one current route eliminates ambiguous old/new activation

relations.rationale: only the active installed projection may become globally authoritative

commitments.trigger: the staged installation has activated and passed installed checks
commitments.validation_boundary: registry currentness, exact route count, and old-route non-authority

commitments.expected_result: commit, tag, release, docs, and active installation share the Logic Writing v2.0.0 release identity
commitments.expected_terminal: public v2.0.0 release with immutable prior history or visible blocked publication
commitments.failure_boundary: no release while validation, installation, privacy, or fresh-clone evidence is stale or missing
commitments.label: publish the verified Logic Writing release
commitments.rationale: a single identity prevents a green claim for a different source snapshot

relations.rationale: publication consumes current validation, install, and routing evidence

commitments.trigger: the installed, routed replacement is current and release candidate checks pass
commitments.validation_boundary: commit, tag, release, VERSION, README, install, and fresh clone match

commitments.expected_result: verified backups and healthy replacement precede local removal, travel-then-storyline GitHub privatization, and an explicit no-deletion terminal
commitments.expected_terminal: travel-story-planner and storyline-design-skill are private with authenticated visibility and anonymous-inaccessibility receipts while Logic Writing remains healthy
commitments.failure_boundary: never privatize before recovery, release, install, routing, residual, and post-first-privatization health gates pass; never privatize Storyline before Travel; never issue repository deletion
commitments.label: retire Travel and Storyline predecessor skills and public repository surfaces sequentially
commitments.rationale: public-surface retirement is safe only after an independently recoverable replacement exists, and irreversible deletion remains user-owned

relations.rationale: the replacement release must exist before old authorities are removed
installed representative operation evidence must remain healthy across retirement

commitments.trigger: the replacement release, installation, routing, backups, and residual audit are current
commitments.validation_boundary: backup restore, zero active residuals, Travel-then-Storyline authenticated PRIVATE plus anonymous 404 receipts, replacement health rechecks, rollback, and deletion prohibition

commitments.expected_result: fiction planning or prose preserves story contribution, reader state, promises, continuity, voice, real manuscript identity, semantic review, and model-prose binding at the requested depth
commitments.expected_terminal: current bounded fiction artifact, explicit human review, partial result, or visible block
commitments.failure_boundary: compact evidence cannot close longform prose and attractive prose cannot replace missing model, binding, continuity, Guard, or actual-artifact evidence
commitments.label: produce a model-bound fiction artifact
commitments.rationale: fiction has route-specific narrative semantics that cannot be collapsed into generic prose quality

relations.rationale: fiction begins only after terminal deliverable routing

commitments.trigger: the terminal deliverable is fiction planning, prose, revision, or audit
commitments.validation_boundary: fiction native regression, exact known-bad family, actual manuscript identity, and route-specific semantic review

commitments.expected_result: a source-time-aware, feasible, traveler-fit, fallback-aware guide is projected through the shared reader kernel and reverse-reviewed from actual bytes
commitments.expected_terminal: initial-plan, bookable, day-of, downgraded, human-review, or blocked guide boundary
commitments.failure_boundary: story polish cannot strengthen source freshness, weather, alert, feasibility, fit, lodging, fallback, or artifact identity status
commitments.label: produce a verified traveler-native guide
commitments.rationale: travel requires live-world, feasibility, fit, and fallback semantics beyond generic narrative projection

relations.rationale: travel begins only after terminal deliverable routing
travel consumes SourceGuard, WorldGuard, TraceGuard, and LogicGuard evidence

commitments.trigger: the terminal deliverable is a substantive itinerary, route, lodging, fit recommendation, or traveler guide
commitments.validation_boundary: travel native regression, exact time/feasibility/fallback cases, and actual-guide reverse review

commitments.expected_result: important planned rows and reader-facing units bind to the same current artifact identity with concrete reader-state handoffs and route-specific extensions
commitments.expected_terminal: current binding and reverse audit or visible drift
commitments.failure_boundary: generic handoffs, unbound prose, unrealized rows, duplicate binding without changed effect, and stale artifact hashes cannot pass
commitments.label: bind model work to the actual reader artifact
commitments.rationale: shared model-to-reader evidence improves every route without transferring route-native semantics

relations.rationale: binding consumes the route-neutral reader contract

commitments.trigger: a selected route projects model work into a reader-facing artifact
commitments.validation_boundary: shared writing regression, actual artifact fingerprint, and route-specific reverse audit

ledger.project_boundary: Logic Writing agent operation plus validation, install, release, and predecessor retirement
ledger.rationale: register the complete promise set before implementation and update evidence bindings only from current owner receipts

source_surfaces.label: OpenSpec capability and verification contract
source_surfaces.rationale: the approved change defines behavior before implementation
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: strict OpenSpec validation and model-test alignment

source_surfaces.label: single Logic Writing skill entrypoint
source_surfaces.rationale: one public skill entry delegates to one selected route
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: installed route smoke and Primary Path Authority

source_surfaces.label: release and retirement process contract
source_surfaces.rationale: the process surface defines irreversible side-effect gates
source_surfaces.validation_boundary: DevelopmentProcessFlow plus release and retirement receipts

ledger.validation_boundary: structure is current now; evidence remains explicitly missing until implementation owners produce receipts
